mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 4.5%, it signals that the Federal Reserve is actively tightening monetary policy. Higher rates compress equity valuations via discount rate increases and slow future economic activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If in quarters where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the median 60-day forward return of the S&P 500 is positive or the rank IC between a binary tightening indicator and forward returns is below 0.03 over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and supports equity prices. This signal is always active and does not require a threshold condition.
failure_condition: If the trailing 12-month growth rate of RSAFS is negative and the mean 20-day forward S&P 500 return remains positive, or if the correlation between RSAFS month-over-month change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p>0.10) over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5% and payroll employment is rising strongly (more than 200,000 per month), the labor market is exceptionally tight. This increases wage inflation fears and the probability of faster Fed tightening, which depresses equity prices in the near term.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0 and PAYEMS > 200 (monthly change in thousands)
failure_condition: If in months meeting the trigger condition, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive, or if the interaction term of UNRATE and PAYEMS in a linear regression does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient (p<0.10) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 2 million units (seasonally adjusted annual rate) signal a softening housing market, which historically precedes broader economic weakness and lower corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If in quarters where HOUST falls below 2000, the median 60-day forward S&P 500 return is greater than zero, or if the binary signal (below threshold) has an information coefficient below 0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A moderate and steady rise in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals healthy demand and pricing power for firms, supporting nominal earnings growth and positive equity returns. This relationship is always active under the assumption of moderate inflation.
failure_condition: If the trailing 12-month CPI growth exceeds 3% and the 20-day forward return is negative on average, or if the rank correlation between CPI month-over-month change and 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p<0.05) over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When interest rates are elevated (federal funds rate above 4%) but retail sales are growing at a healthy pace (year-over-year growth above 5%), the combination indicates a resilient economy that can absorb higher rates, supporting continued equity gains in the near term.
failure_condition: If in periods meeting the interaction condition, the mean 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative or not significantly different from zero, or the interaction term in a regression of returns on the two signals is not positive and significant (p<0.10) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already low (below 5%), any further month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate is a leading indicator of labor market deterioration and future economic slowdown, causing short-term negative equity returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If in weeks where UNRATE increases by 0.2pp or more, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is positive, or if the hit rate of negative returns in those weeks is not significantly greater than 50% (binomial test p>0.05) over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its recent plateau signals weakening manufacturing activity, which often precedes broader economic contractions and lower corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If in quarters where INDPRO falls below 111, the median 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive, or the binary signal (below threshold) has no significant negative correlation with future returns (Spearman rho > -0.05) over any 4-year rolling window.
